items.title: Frequently Asked Questions
items.text:
What is Sliver-Weather?

Sliver-Weather is a Docker Compose deployment that showcases the Sliver C2 framework for cybersecurity training and demonstration purposes. It includes a weather website as a cover application, nginx as a reverse proxy, and a Sliver C2 server for command and control operations. This environment allows users to test and demonstrate Sliver's capabilities in a controlled setting.

What is the purpose of the weather website in Sliver-Weather?

The weather website is a cover application that provides a legitimate-looking service, making it more difficult to detect the Sliver C2 server. It serves as a distraction and helps to blend in with normal network traffic.

How do I install Sliver-Weather?

To install Sliver-Weather, you'll need to have Docker and Docker Compose installed on your system. Then, follow these steps:

  1. Clone the Sliver-Weather repository: git clone https://github.com/your-repo/sliver-weather.git
  2. Navigate to the repository: cd sliver-weather
  3. Build and start the containers: docker-compose up -d
How do I access the weather website?

Once the containers are running, you can access the weather website by visiting http://localhost:8080 in your web browser. If you're running Sliver-Weather on a remote server, replace localhost with the server's IP address or domain name.

How do I configure Sliver-Weather?

Configuration files are stored in the config directory. You can modify the sliver.yaml file to change Sliver's settings, such as the server's IP address and port number.

# config/sliver.yaml
server:
  ip: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8081
How do I use Sliver-Weather?

To use Sliver-Weather, you'll need to start the Sliver C2 server and connect to it using the Sliver client. Here are the basic steps:

  1. Start the Sliver C2 server: docker-compose exec sliver-server sliver server
  2. Connect to the server using the Sliver client: sliver client --server <server-ip>:8081
I'm experiencing issues with Sliver-Weather. How do I troubleshoot?

If you're experiencing issues with Sliver-Weather, check the container logs for error messages:

docker-compose logs

You can also try restarting the containers:

docker-compose restart

If you're still having trouble, consult the Sliver documentation or seek help from the Sliver community.

Can I contribute to Sliver-Weather?

Yes! Sliver-Weather is an open-source project, and contributions are welcome. You can contribute by submitting bug reports, feature requests, or code patches. Please see the CONTRIBUTING.md file for more information.

How do I update Sliver-Weather?

To update Sliver-Weather, navigate to the repository and pull the latest changes:

git pull origin main

Then, rebuild and restart the containers:

docker-compose up -d --build
What are the system requirements for running Sliver-Weather?

Sliver-Weather requires a system with Docker and Docker Compose installed. The minimum system requirements are:

  • 2 CPU cores
  • 4 GB RAM
  • 10 GB disk space
Is Sliver-Weather suitable for production use?

No, Sliver-Weather is intended for cybersecurity training and demonstration purposes only. It is not suitable for production use, as it is not designed to be secure or reliable.

How do I secure Sliver-Weather?

Sliver-Weather is not designed to be secure, but you can take steps to harden it:

  • Use a secure password for the Sliver C2 server
  • Limit access to the Sliver C2 server and weather website
  • Use a reverse proxy to hide the Sliver C2 server
Can I use Sliver-Weather with other tools?

Yes, Sliver-Weather can be used with other tools, such as Burp Suite or ZAP. You can configure these tools to interact with the Sliver C2 server and weather website.

Where can I find more information about Sliver?

You can find more information about Sliver on the Sliver GitHub page.

items.title: Prerequisites
items.text:
• Docker Engine 20.10+ and Docker Compose 1.29+ (or any Compose plugin supporting version 3.8)
  • Git
  • 4 GB free disk space for images and build cache
  • Ports 80 and 443 available on the host (or override with NGINX_HTTP_PORT / NGINX_HTTPS_PORT)

Sources: docker-compose.yml:1

items.title: Installation
items.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/JongoDB/sliver-weather.git
cd sliver-weather

  2. Create a minimal .env file: 

    cat <<EOF > .env
DOMAIN=localhost
USE_TLS=0
NGINX_HTTP_PORT=80
NGINX_HTTPS_PORT=443
SLIVER_HTTP_PORT=8080
NETWORK_NAME=demo_net
EOF

  3. Build and start the stack: 

    docker compose up --build -d

The first build downloads ~1 GB of image layers and compiles the React weather app.

Sources: docker-compose.yml:1-59

items.title: Quick Start
items.text:
1. Verify containers: 

    docker compose ps
    Expected output: 
    NAME      STATUS          PORTS
nginx     Up 30s          0.0.0.0:80->80, 0.0.0.0:443->443
sliver    Up 30s          80/tcp, 443/tcp
weather   Up 30s          5000/tcp

  2. Open the weather cover site: 

    http://localhost/weather
    The SPA loads from weather/src/main.jsx and serves at /weather basename.

  3. Attach to the Sliver console: 

    docker compose exec -it sliver /opt/sliver-server
    An HTTP listener is already running on port 8080 inside the container.

Sources: weather/src/main.jsx:1-11, sliver/Dockerfile:40-57

items.title: Welcome to Sliver Weather
items.text: Sliver Weather is a special kind of website that helps cybersecurity professionals train and test their skills in a safe and controlled environment. It's not just a weather website - it's a tool for learning and demonstrating how to protect computer systems from cyber threats.

items.title: Who is it for?
items.text:
Sliver Weather is designed for cybersecurity students, trainers, and professionals who want to practice their skills in a realistic and secure way. It's perfect for:

  • Cybersecurity training and education
  • Penetration testing and vulnerability assessment
  • Red team and blue team exercises
  • Anyone interested in learning about cybersecurity and how to protect computer systems

items.title: Main Capabilities
items.text:
Here are some of the key features of Sliver Weather:

  • A weather website that looks and feels like a real one, but with a secret: it's actually a cover for a command and control (C2) server
  • A C2 server that allows you to simulate cyber threats and test your defenses
  • A Docker Compose deployment that makes it easy to set up and run the environment
  • A reverse proxy (nginx) that helps keep everything secure and organized

items.title: Getting Started
items.text: Don't worry if you're not a tech expert - Sliver Weather is designed to be easy to use. Just follow the instructions in the documentation, and you'll be up and running in no time. With Sliver Weather, you can start learning and practicing your cybersecurity skills in a safe and controlled environment.

items.title: server.js
items.text:
Express server that orchestrates weather data retrieval by exposing /api/weather endpoints, geocoding city names via Open-Meteo, and caching responses in memory for 60-second TTL. Exports an Express app instance configured with static file serving and JSON middleware, consumed by the frontend to fetch current and 10-day forecasts in Fahrenheit. Bridges the browser to external geocoding and weather APIs while shielding them behind a unified internal cache layer.
